description: AI Workload Capacity Planning — stack-level ordering: Nodes → Platform → Namespace → Workload → Inference Capacity

panels.description: At-a-glance device status: blue = idle, green = has active workload
panels.title: Device Workload Map

panels.description: Per-node GPU device inventory. Sorted by compute utilization.
panels.title: Device Inventory & Status — $hostname

panels.description: Node CPU and RAM from kube-state-metrics / cAdvisor. Node label must match $hostname.
panels.title: Node Resources — $hostname

panels.description: Active deployments per node (kube-state-metrics ReplicaSet owner join)
panels.title: Deployments on Node — $hostname

panels.description: Power usage over time per device (filtered by selected nodes)
panels.title: Power Usage by Device

panels.description: Temperature over time per device (filtered by selected nodes)
panels.title: Temperature by Device

panels.description: Tensor core utilization per workload
panels.title: Tensor Utilization by Workload

panels.description: SM Clock frequency by GPU model
panels.title: SM Clock by GPU Model

panels.description: Top 10 GPU/MIG devices by GR engine active % (pure compute, no composite formula)
panels.title: Top 10 Devices by Compute %

panels.description: GR engine active % per GPU/MIG device on this node
panels.title: Compute % by Device — $hostname

panels.description: Total GPU memory capacity across all devices
panels.title: Total Memory Capacity

panels.description: Total GPU memory currently in use
panels.title: Memory In Use

panels.description: Average VRAM utilization across all devices
panels.title: Avg Memory Utilization

panels.description: Percentage of devices at risk of OOM (>85% VRAM)
panels.title: OOM Risk %

panels.description: Average temperature across all GPUs
panels.title: Avg Temperature

panels.description: Average power usage per device
panels.title: Avg Power per Device

panels.description: VRAM utilization per device on this node. Whole GPUs and MIG instances shown separately to avoid legend clutter.
panels.title: Memory Utilization % — $hostname

panels.description: VRAM used per namespace on this node
panels.title: VRAM by Namespace — $hostname

panels.description: VRAM used by workloads over time
panels.title: Workload Memory Over Time

panels.description: Node CPU (millicores) and RAM (MiB) usage per Kubernetes deployment. Source: kube-state-metrics + cAdvisor — not DCGM GPU metrics.

targets.expr:
sum by (deployment, namespace) (
  rate(container_cpu_usage_seconds_total{container!="", container!="POD"}[5m]) * 1000
* on(pod, namespace) group_left(deployment)
label_replace(
  kube_pod_owner{owner_kind="ReplicaSet"},
  "deployment", "$1", "owner_name", "(.+)-[^-]+"
)
)

panels.title: CPU & RAM by Deployment

panels.description: Per-workload GPU usage. VRAM shown as Used/Total (MiB). GPU column removed; Node and GPU Model added.
panels.title: Workload GPU Usage

panels.description: GPU compute activity per workload on this node (GR engine active %)
panels.title: Workload Compute % — $hostname

panels.description: Output tokens generated per second. Primary throughput signal for LLM inference capacity.
panels.title: Gen Tokens/s

panels.description: Requests currently being decoded. Correlate with GPU Compute % to identify saturation mode.
panels.title: Requests Running

panels.description: Requests queued in the vLLM scheduler. Growing = backpressure. Cross with GPU % to find root cause.
panels.title: Requests Waiting

panels.description: GPU KV cache utilization (vLLM). >85% → OOM risk, triggers CPU swapping and preemption.
panels.title: KV Cache Pressure

panels.description: Requests preempted to CPU KV cache. Non-zero = GPU KV cache overflow. Degrades latency significantly.
panels.title: Requests Swapped

panels.description: Fraction of prefix cache lookups that hit. Higher = less redundant prefill work. No-data = no prefix queries in window or vLLM <v0.14.
panels.title: Prefix Cache Hit Rate

panels.description:
Saturation mode diagnostic — read the relationship between the two lines:
  GPU ↑ + Queue ↑  →  compute saturation: add capacity or reduce concurrency
  GPU ↓ + Queue ↑  →  KV cache / scheduling bottleneck, not compute
  GPU ↑ + Queue ↓  →  single large request dominating (consider MIG)
  GPU ↓ + Queue ↓  →  healthy idle
panels.title: GPU Compute % vs Queue Depth

panels.description:
Hardware VRAM utilization (DCGM) vs inference KV cache utilization (vLLM) on the same scale.
  Gap = model weights + CUDA context + activations (fixed per model)
  Both rising together  →  OOM risk: reduce max_num_seqs or context window
  KV high, VRAM steady  →  KV cache dominating: long sequences or large batches
  VRAM high, KV low     →  model weights fill VRAM with little room for context
panels.title: VRAM % (hardware) vs KV Cache % (vLLM)

panels.description:
Output token generation rate divided by active GPU count (DCGM). Unit-economics metric for LLM inference.
  Declining trend         →  GPUs underutilized (idle replicas, cold starts, scheduling gaps)
  Flat/rising + queue ↑  →  true compute saturation — fleet expansion needed
  Spiky pattern           →  bursty workload or model loading latency
panels.title: Generation Tokens / Active GPU / s

title: GPU Capacity Planning Dashboard
